title: Flock Safety Emails Dec 2023 - Feb 2026

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Flock Safety Emails Dec 2023 - Feb 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

All all agency emails (including any attachments) that contain a Flock Safety email address  <*@flocksafety.com> sent or received, including CC & BCC fields, between 12/1/2023 and the date this request is processed (inclusive of those dates).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Zeke Martin

communications.subject: Flock Safety ALPR Audits Q1
communications.communication:
Points 1 and 2.
            Records that might exist responsive to this inquiry are exempt. FOIA provides that "(d-7) Information gathered or records created from the use of automatic license plate readers in connection with Section 2-130 of the Illinois Vehicle Code" are exempt.



February 20, 2026
To Whom It May Concern:
Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:
1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2026 to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.
2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2026 to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization's Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.
Please provide the audit data in a structured format such as .csv, .xls, or another machine-readable database filetype.
The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.
In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.
Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.
Sincerely,
Zeke Martin

communications.communication:
A blanket request for "any" or "all" emails with that address over a three year period is categorical and evaluation of potential response unduly burdensome.  Please advise if you wish to narrow down to a specific date, or week, or alternatively a specific individual's email (versus ending email address).   If we do not receive a narrowed response within 5 days, we will presume you are not interested in doing so and you should treat this response as a denial.


February 20, 2026
To Whom It May Concern:
Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:
All all agency emails (including any attachments) that contain a Flock Safety email address <*@flocksafety.com<mailto:*@flocksafety.com>> sent or received, including CC & BCC fields, between 12/1/2023 and the date this request is processed (inclusive of those dates).
The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.
In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.
Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.
Sincerely,
Zeke Martin

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Flock Safety Emails Dec 2023 - Feb 2026
communications.communication:
Hello!

As these are two separate FOIA requests I will only be responding to "Flock Safety Emails Dec 2023 - Feb 2026".

As for this request here is a narrowed request made under the provisions of Illinois Freedom of Information Act. for the following records:

Copies of all emails received by your agency from any sender email address containing the domain @flocksafety.com (**but excluding emails from <hotlist@flocksafety.com>) between 10/1/2025 and the date this request is processed (inclusive of those dates). 

This drastically limits the scope of search from 25 months to 4 months.

This request includes but is not limited to:

1. Emails received by any employee official or representative of Sterling Police Department using an agency issued email account 

2. All attachments included with those emails 

3. Related metadata sufficient to identify sender recipient (To:, CC:, BCC:), date and time of receipt, and subject line.

This can likely be done by searching "@flocksafety.com" in your search, and limiting or redacting any with "hotlist" at the beginning. 

And as for the "Flock Safety ALPR Audits 2026 Q1 (Sterling Police Department Illinois)" request, I am happy to talk about that in its proper channel.

With thanks,
 
Zeke Martin

title: National Institute of Diabetes legal name documents

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: National Institute of Diabetes legal name documents
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The March 13 memo informing institute employees that they can only use “full legal name” in email signatures and cannot use pronouns or "nicknames". I also request any other agency memos, guidance documents or communications regarding this new policy or any other policy implemented in response to Executive Order 14168, known as "Defending Women from Gender Ideology Extremism and Restoring Biological Truth to the Federal Government."

I request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, processed by a representative of the news media/press and is made in the process of news gathering and not for commercial usage.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ileana Garnand

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] Freedom of Information Act Request: National Institute of Diabetes legal name documents
communications.communication:
Good afternoon Ileana,

We are in receipt of your FOIA request below and need more information/clarification before we can begin processing your request. First, is your request specific to the National Institute of Diabetes & Digestive & Kidney Diseases (NIDDK) or all of NIH? It mentions NIDDK at the top but we just want to make sure we are searching the correct offices for the documents you have requested. Also, it would be helpful to have direct contact information (email, phone number, address) because we have run into issues in the past with getting communications to requesters through the MuckRock platform.

Thank you,
NHLBI FOIA Service Center

title: CPRA Request: Flock Safety ALPR Records

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: CPRA Request: Flock Safety ALPR Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Unless a different timeframe is specified below, this request covers records from January 1, 2024, to the date of this request.

I. ALPR Data Intake from Specified Agencies
1. All records identifying the law enforcement agencies that currently transmit, upload, or otherwise provide Automated License Plate Reader (ALPR) data to NCRIC, including the method of transmission for each agency (e.g., automatic real-time feed, batch upload, or query-based access).
2. All Memoranda of Understanding (MOUs), data-sharing agreements, network-participation terms, or other written agreements between NCRIC and the following agencies governing the sharing or receipt of ALPR data:
a. San Mateo County Sheriff's Office (SMCSO)
b. Belmont Police Department (Belmont, California)
c. Burlingame Police Department (Burlingame, California)
d. Daly City Police Department (Daly City, California)
e. San Bruno Police Department (San Bruno, California)
3. Records identifying the volume of ALPR plate reads received by NCRIC from each of the five agencies listed in Item 2, aggregated by month.
4. Records identifying the ALPR technology vendors or platforms (e.g., Flock Safety, Vigilant Solutions/Motorola, Rekor, etc.) from which NCRIC receives or ingests data, and whether the data from each of the five agencies listed in Item 2 arrives through a vendor-operated platform or through a direct agency feed.

II. Query and Access Logs
5. All existing audit or access logs generated by NCRIC's ALPR system(s) documenting queries or searches of ALPR data originating from each of the following agencies, with results provided separately for each agency:
a. San Mateo County Sheriff's Office
b. Belmont Police Department
c. Burlingame Police Department
d. Daly City Police Department
e. San Bruno Police Department
For each query record, I request: the date and time of the query, the agency or unit of the individual conducting the query, and the stated purpose or justification for the query as recorded in the system. I request that personally identifiable information of non-employee query subjects be redacted rather than the records withheld in their entirety.
6. Records identifying the total number of queries made against NCRIC's ALPR database(s) by all users, aggregated by month, and broken down by querying agency where such records exist.
7. A list of all agencies, units, or task forces (local, state, and federal) whose personnel currently have authorized access to query NCRIC's ALPR database(s).

III. Downstream Sharing and Federal Access
8. All records documenting instances in which ALPR data held by NCRIC (including data originating from any of the five agencies listed in Item 2) was provided to, accessed by, or queried by personnel of any federal agency, including but not limited to:
a. U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE)
b. Homeland Security Investigations (HSI)
c. U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP)
d. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI)
e. Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA)
f. Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives (ATF)
g. U.S. Marshals Service
9. All records documenting instances in which intelligence products, analytical reports, or investigative leads derived in whole or in part from ALPR data were disseminated by NCRIC to any federal agency or to personnel assigned to a federal task force, including joint task forces operating under federal direction.
10. Any policies, directives, or written guidance that govern or restrict the downstream dissemination of ALPR data or ALPR-derived intelligence by NCRIC to federal agencies, including any restrictions imposed by the originating local agency (e.g., any of the five agencies listed in Item 2).
11. Any policies or directives addressing NCRIC's obligations under California's sanctuary or data-privacy laws (including the California Values Act, SB 54, and Civil Code § 1798.90.51 et seq.) as they relate to the sharing of ALPR data with federal immigration enforcement agencies.

IV. Policy, Retention, and Oversight
12. NCRIC's current ALPR Usage and Privacy Policy, as required by California Civil Code § 1798.90.51, including data retention schedules.
13. All annual audit reports of NCRIC's ALPR program required by California Civil Code § 1798.90.53, from January 1, 2023, to the present.
14. Records identifying the current data retention period for ALPR data held by NCRIC, and any records reflecting changes to the retention period during the timeframe of this request.
15. Records of any unauthorized access, data breaches, policy violations, or security incidents involving ALPR data held by NCRIC.
16. Records of any complaints or inquiries received from members of the public, advocacy organizations, or elected officials regarding NCRIC's ALPR data practices.

V. Hot Lists and Alerting
17. A list of all categories of "hot lists" (e.g., stolen vehicles, AMBER alerts, BOLO lists) currently integrated into or compared against NCRIC's ALPR database(s), including the source of each hot list.
18. Any records describing the use of custom or locally generated hot lists, including the criteria or authorization process for creating such lists.

VI. Governance and Oversight Structure
19. The names and titles of NCRIC personnel responsible for administering, overseeing, or auditing the ALPR program.
20. Agendas, minutes, or summaries of any meetings of NCRIC's Privacy, Civil Rights, and Civil Liberties Advisory Committee (or equivalent oversight body) at which ALPR data practices were discussed, from January 1, 2023, to the present.

If you determine that any portion of the requested records is exempt from disclosure, I ask that you redact only those portions and provide the remainder of the record. For any redaction or denial, please cite the specific legal exemption justifying the withholding, as required by Government Code § 7922.540.

Given that this request is made in the public interest by a student journalist writing for Scot Scoop, I respectfully request a waiver or reduction of any fees associated with this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Maximus Kwan

communications.subject: Re: California Public Records Act Request: CPRA Request: Flock Safety ALPR Records
communications.communication:
Note: the Request uses terms that imply large-scale data movement or duplication such as "intake" and "ingest" and "receive" and "transmit" and "upload" and "held" when it should be clarified that NCRIC only has ad-hoc query-based access to ALPR data hosted within the Flock and Vigilant platforms, through standard browser-based interfaces provided by the vendor, with the breadth of potential matches of those queries limited by the sharing rules, permissions, and retention policies established and directly controlled by the other agencies who share their data.

  1.  All records identifying the law enforcement agencies that currently transmit, upload, or otherwise provide Automated License Plate Reader (ALPR) data to NCRIC, including the method of transmission for each agency (e.g., automatic real-time feed, batch upload, or query-based access). See attached "Agencies sharing their Flock ALPR data with NCRIC"
  2.  All Memoranda of Understanding (MOUs), data-sharing agreements, network-participation terms, or other written agreements between NCRIC and the following agencies governing the sharing or receipt of ALPR data. See attached data sharing Memorandum of Understandings and NCRIC ALPR Policy.
  3.  Records identifying the volume of ALPR plate reads received by NCRIC from each of the five agencies listed in Item 2, aggregated by month. No such records exist. NCRIC has no visibility into the activity of other agency/customer ALPR devices. NCRIC receives no ALPR data from other agencies. NCRIC only has query-based access to ALPR data in Flock where the Flock customer agency has elected to allow access.
  4.  Records identifying the ALPR technology vendors or platforms (e.g., Flock Safety, Vigilant Solutions/Motorola, Rekor, etc.) from which NCRIC receives or ingests data, and whether the data from each of the five agencies listed in Item 2 arrives through a vendor-operated platform or through a direct agency feed. NCRIC has ad-hoc query-based access to ALPR data in Flock and Vigilant through standard vendor-operated interfaces.
  5.  All existing audit or access logs generated by NCRIC's ALPR system(s) documenting queries or searches of ALPR data originating from each of the following agencies, with results provided separately for each agency. It is unclear whether this request is for queries/searches conducted by users from the listed agencies, or searches that may have included data from the listed agencies. For either, that information may be available from the agency but NCRIC does not have records responsive to this request.
  6.  Records identifying the total number of queries made against NCRIC's ALPR database(s) by all users, aggregated by month, and broken down by querying agency where such records exist. No such summary report exists.
  7.  A list of all agencies, units, or task forces (local, state, and federal) whose personnel currently have authorized access to query NCRIC's ALPR database(s). See attached "Agencies NCRIC shares its Flock ALPR data with" for other Flock customer agencies granted searchability to our records.
  8.  All records documenting instances in which ALPR data held by NCRIC (including data originating from any of the five agencies listed in Item 2) was provided to, accessed by, or queried by personnel of any federal agency, including but not limited to...No such records exist. NCRIC does not share ALPR data or provide access to ALPR systems to any federal personnel.
  9.  All records documenting instances in which intelligence products, analytical reports, or investigative leads derived in whole or in part from ALPR data were disseminated by NCRIC to any federal agency or to personnel assigned to a federal task force, including joint task forces operating under federal direction. No such records exist. NCRIC does not share ALPR data or provide access to ALPR systems to any federal personnel.
  10. Any policies, directives, or written guidance that govern or restrict the downstream dissemination of ALPR data or ALPR-derived intelligence by NCRIC to federal agencies, including any restrictions imposed by the originating local agency (e.g., any of the five agencies listed in Item 2). See attached NCRIC ALPR Policy and CA DOJ Memorandum regarding SB34 and SB54
  11. Any policies or directives addressing NCRIC's obligations under California's sanctuary or data-privacy laws (including the California Values Act, SB 54, and Civil Code § 1798.90.51 et seq.) as they relate to the sharing of ALPR data with federal immigration enforcement agencies. See attached NCRIC ALPR Policy and CA DOJ Memorandum regarding SB34 and SB54
  12. NCRIC's current ALPR Usage and Privacy Policy, as required by California Civil Code § 1798.90.51, including data retention schedules. See attached NCRIC ALPR Policy
  13. All annual audit reports of NCRIC's ALPR program required by California Civil Code § 1798.90.53, from January 1, 2023, to the present. ALPR audits were conducted in September 2025, December 2025, and February 2026. All had no findings, including 100% compliance with no participation from federal partners or agencies outside California. Records from prior audits were unable to be located.
  14. Records identifying the current data retention period for ALPR data held by NCRIC, and any records reflecting changes to the retention period during the timeframe of this request. See attached NCRIC ALPR Policy. The current retention period is 30 days
  15. Records of any unauthorized access, data breaches, policy violations, or security incidents involving ALPR data held by NCRIC. No such records exist.
  16. Records of any complaints or inquiries received from members of the public, advocacy organizations, or elected officials regarding NCRIC's ALPR data practices. No such records exist.
  17. A list of all categories of "hot lists" (e.g., stolen vehicles, AMBER alerts, BOLO lists) currently integrated into or compared against NCRIC's ALPR database(s), including the source of each hot list. "Felony Vehicles" and "Stolen Vehicles" from the "California SVS"
  18. Any records describing the use of custom or locally generated hot lists, including the criteria or authorization process for creating such lists. No such records exist.
  19. The names and titles of NCRIC personnel responsible for administering, overseeing, or auditing the ALPR program. Brian Rodrigues, NCRIC Assistant Deputy Director
  20. Agendas, minutes, or summaries of any meetings of NCRIC's Privacy, Civil Rights, and Civil Liberties Advisory Committee (or equivalent oversight body) at which ALPR data practices were discussed, from January 1, 2023, to the present. See attached "Exec Board Meeting Agenda 2024-09-17" and "NC HIDTA NCRIC Policy Procedure Section 16 ALPR June 2025"

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Jimmy Kimmel Live FCC Complaints
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All complaints received regarding Jimmy Kimmel Live! on ABC from September 1, 2025, until the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jed Rosenzweig

title: Organized Retail Crime Task Force: Auror

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Organized Retail Crime Task Force: Auror
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records related to the Organized Retail Crime Theft Task Force announced on June 23, 2022, specifically concerning the implementation, use, and discussion of Auror's crime intelligence platform.

1. All contracts, MOUs, RFPs, or service agreements between the AG's Office and Auror Inc. (auror.co) dated June 23, 2022, to present
1a. Amendments, scope-of-work documents, or SLA agreements related to Auror's services
1b. Invoices, payment receipts, or procurement documents for Auror software/services

2. Budget allocations specific to Auror platform implementation
2a. Documentation of federal/state grants used to fund Auror-related expenditures

3. Training manuals, guides, or protocols for Auror platform use by task force members
3a. Video recordings of Auror software training sessions
3b. Email communications regarding training schedules or materials

4. All incident reports, intelligence briefings, or case files generated through Auror's platform
4a. Spreadsheets/databases tracking organized retail crime incidents processed via Auror
4b. Performance metrics/reports evaluating Auror's effectiveness (2022-2024)

5. Records of technical malfunctions, data inaccuracies, or security breaches involving Auror
5a. Formal complaints from task force members or partner agencies about Auror's system
5b. Internal audits or third-party assessments of Auror's compliance with state data laws

6. Emails/memos between AG staff and Auror representatives (June 23, 2022-present)
6a. Meeting minutes where Auror platform use was discussed
6b. Correspondence with federal partners (FBI, DHS) regarding Auror data sharing

These records are requested from June 23, 2022, to present; Electronic copies are preferred in their original formats wherever possible, and commonly readable formats such as pdf, mp4, png where conversion is required.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robin Monks

communications.communication:

Please reference that number on any future correspondence regarding this request. You requested the following: Records related to the Organized Retail Crime Theft Task Force announced on June 23, 2022, specifically concerning the implementation, use, and discussion of Auror's crime intelligence platform. 
1. All contracts, MOUs, RFPs, or service agreements between the AG's Office and Auror Inc. (auror.co) dated June 23, 2022, to present

1b. Invoices, payment receipts, or procurement documents for Auror software/services 
2. Budget allocations specific to Auror platform implementation 
2a. Documentation of federal/state grants used to fund Auror-related expenditures 
3. Training manuals, guides, or protocols for Auror platform use by task force members 
3a. Video recordings of Auror software training sessions 
3b. Email communications regarding training schedules or materials 
4. All incident reports, intelligence briefings, or case files generated through Auror's platform 
4a. Spreadsheets/databases tracking organized retail crime incidents processed via Auror 
4b. Performance metrics/reports evaluating Auror's effectiveness (2022-2024) 
5. Records of technical malfunctions, data inaccuracies, or security breaches involving Auror 
5a. Formal complaints from task force members or partner agencies about Auror's system 
5b. Internal audits or third-party assessments of Auror's compliance with state data laws 
6. Emails/memos between AG staff and Auror representatives (June 23, 2022-present) 
6a. Meeting minutes where Auror platform use was discussed 
6b. Correspondence with federal partners (FBI, DHS) regarding Auror data sharing 
These records are requested from June 23, 2022, to present; Electronic copies are preferred in their original formats wherever possible, and commonly readable formats such as pdf, mp4, png where conversion is required. 
The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 
In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. We are searching our records to determine the location, nature, and amount of records, if any, that may be responsive to your request. If responsive records are found, I estimate I can make available an installment of records on or before April 2, 2025. If records are ready prior to that time, I will notify you. If this time assessment requires adjustment, I will also advise you of that. Please contact me within 30 days via email or at Office of the Attorney General, Public Records & Constituent Services, P.O. Box 40100, Olympia, WA 98504-0100, if you have any questions. Thank you.

4a. Spreadsheets/databases tracking organized retail crime incidents processed via Auror 
4b. Performance metrics/reports evaluating Auror's effectiveness (2022-2024)” Clarification Question: When you ask for “incident reports” are you requesting the numerous individual reports written in Auror documenting each alleged criminal act that occurred at the retail establishment (e.g. each report when an individual stole something from a store)? I’m looking for instances when individual reports about an alleged criminal act in Auror were made by a member of your department or have been used or referenced by your department as part of your work. Thank you,
Robin

communications.subject: PRR Time Estimate Update - Monks - PRR-2025-0182
communications.communication:
Dear Robin Monks, Thank you for reviewing records via Box. This correspondence is to update you on your public records request. You requested the following: Records related to the Organized Retail Crime Theft Task Force announced on June 23, 2022, specifically concerning the implementation, use, and discussion of Auror's crime intelligence platform. 
1. All contracts, MOUs, RFPs, or service agreements between the AG's Office and Auror Inc. (auror.co) dated June 23, 2022, to present 
1a. Amendments, scope-of-work documents, or SLA agreements related to Auror's services 
1b. Invoices, payment receipts, or procurement documents for Auror software/services 
2. Budget allocations specific to Auror platform implementation 
2a. Documentation of federal/state grants used to fund Auror-related expenditures 
3. Training manuals, guides, or protocols for Auror platform use by task force members 
3a. Video recordings of Auror software training sessions 
3b. Email communications regarding training schedules or materials 
4. All incident reports, intelligence briefings, or case files generated through Auror's platform 
4a. Spreadsheets/databases tracking organized retail crime incidents processed via Auror 
4b. Performance metrics/reports evaluating Auror's effectiveness (2022-2024) 
5. Records of technical malfunctions, data inaccuracies, or security breaches involving Auror 
5a. Formal complaints from task force members or partner agencies about Auror's system 
5b. Internal audits or third-party assessments of Auror's compliance with state data laws 
6. Emails/memos between AG staff and Auror representatives (June 23, 2022-present) 
6a. Meeting minutes where Auror platform use was discussed 
6b. Correspondence with federal partners (FBI, DHS) regarding Auror data sharing 
These records are requested from June 23, 2022, to present; Electronic copies are preferred in their original formats wherever possible, and commonly readable formats such as pdf, mp4, png where conversion is required. 
The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 
In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. We are continuing review of our records in response to your request. I estimate having another installment of records available on or before August 8, 2025. If records are ready prior to that time, I will notify you. If this time assessment needs adjustment, I will notify you of that as well. Please contact me within 30 days via email or at Office of the Attorney General, Public Records & Constituent Services, P.O. Box 40100, Olympia, WA 98504-0100, if you have any questions. Thank you. Sincerely, 
 Kristin Young 
Kristin Young 
Senior Public Records Officer 
360-586-3568 
Kristin.Young@atg.wa.gov

3a. Video recordings of Auror software training sessions 
3b. Email communications regarding training schedules or materials 
4. All incident reports, intelligence briefings, or case files generated through Auror's platform 
4a. Spreadsheets/databases tracking organized retail crime incidents processed via Auror 
4b. Performance metrics/reports evaluating Auror's effectiveness (2022-2024) 
5. Records of technical malfunctions, data inaccuracies, or security breaches involving Auror 
5a. Formal complaints from task force members or partner agencies about Auror's system 
5b. Internal audits or third-party assessments of Auror's compliance with state data laws 
6. Emails/memos between AG staff and Auror representatives (June 23, 2022-present) 
6a. Meeting minutes where Auror platform use was discussed 
6b. Correspondence with federal partners (FBI, DHS) regarding Auror data sharing 
These records are requested from June 23, 2022, to present; Electronic copies are preferred in their original formats wherever possible, and commonly readable formats such as pdf, mp4, png where conversion is required. 
The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 
In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. An installment of records responsive to your request has been uploaded to Box. The records consist of a 702-page document (RM-00043 thru RM-00744) and 0 (MB) of native data. You may inspect and/or download these records via this link: https://atg-wa.box.com/s/9fx3rleuohaviqf0sfzhx7j0akip6af4 . Please do so within 30 days. Records provided via Box are free of charge. You do not need a Box account to access the records. If a password is required to access the records, one will be provided to you in a separate email. Also uploaded with these responsive documents is a key to the codes indicated as black boxes with a code, e.g., [1a], centered. The key identifies the statutory basis for the exemption as well as a brief explanation for why the exemption applies. As you review the responsive documents, you will be able to refer to the key to identify the exemption and its application. We have made every attempt to apply consistent redactions on documents that contain the same information. In the event you locate documents that contain redactions that are not applied consistently, please let me know. If you prefer to receive these records on CD, USB thumb drive, or paper, there is a charge. Please see the AGO Public Records Fee Schedule for cost information. If you prefer one of these formats, please submit a check or money order to my attention within 30 days, in the amount specified on the Fee Schedule, payable to the Office of the Attorney General, and send it to the address below. Once payment is received, the records will be mailed. Please be advised that if you do not either download these records, access them for inspection, or submit payment for an alternate format on or before September 12, 2025, the request is considered to have been fulfilled and is closed on that date for failure to claim or review records. This means that the Attorney General’s Office will not further address the request, and that is the date from which the Public Records Act’s one-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review starts to run. Please contact me within 30 days via email or at Office of the Attorney General, Public Records & Constituent Services, P.O. Box 40100, Olympia, WA 98504-0100, if you have any questions. Thank you. Sincerely, Kristin Young 
Kristin Young 
Senior Public Records Officer

Kristin.Young@atg.wa.gov

title: State of Virginia Salaries 2025 & 2026

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: State of Virginia Salaries 2025 & 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

State of Virginia 2025 Compensation Data for All Employees (This report can be measured for CY2025, FY2025, or on a Date in Time in 2025, whichever is readily available)
State of Virginia 2026 Compensation Data for All Employees (This report can be measured As of Today's Date)
Please provide the following fields for each employee in the dataset:
Employee # | First Name | Middle Name/Initial | Last Name | Employee Role Title | Position Number | Agency Name | Agency Location | Agency Address | Agency City/State/ZIP | Employee Category | Compensation (Base Pay, Salary, Hourly Rate, Total Pay, etc) | Overtime | Benefits Pay | Date Hired | Date Terminated | FT/PT

Note: If not all fields listed above are releasable as public record or available please disregard those individual fields for this request.

The following fields were provided in a request for 2024 data and the same fields for 2025 will satisfy this request:
Business Unit |	Company |	Agency Description | 	Last Name |	First Name |	Empl Class |	Employee Type |	Job Std Hours |	Position Description |	VPA Only |	Job Code |	Standard Occup Classification |	Salary Plan	Grade |	Step |	Reg/Temp |	Full- Part Time	 |State Comp Rate |	Non State Comp Rate |	Special Comp Rate |	Combined Comp Rate |	Hourly Comp Rate |	FTE |	Company Seniority Date |	Employee State Begin Date


I would prefer the request be filled electronically, by either e-mail attachment or FTP Download if available, otherwise sent through mail via CD/DVD. I would appreciate the request to be filled in an accessible format, including but not limited to: CSV, Excel, Microsoft Access database, or other bulk list file type.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Tyler King.

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Burlington County Sheriff)

communications.subject: New Jersey Open Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Burlington County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Jersey Open Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Recruitment and Hiring (Austin Police Department)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Recruitment and Hiring (Austin Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting records related to the department's efforts to recruit and hire new officers:
Records include digital and physical, released in their native forms (Excel, PDF, etc)

- ad purchases
- contracts with vendors for ad placement, recruitment and hiring 
- market research and data, if collected
- brand and department strategies created by ad agencies or internally
- data related to applications, KPIs of recruitment and hiring campaigns
- PowerPoint or equivalent presentations, including accompanying documents related to recruitment, hiring, and marketing strategies
- Budgets for recruitment and hiring internally, and the total money spent 
- Pay bonuses or financial incentives to retain officers
- Data by year on the number of officers, sworn and non-sworn

Search from 2021 to today's date.
Please provide in rolling production

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

communications.subject: Public Information Act Request :: R158707-052225
communications.communication:
Dear Joey Scott: 
 
Thank you for your interest in the public records of the Austin Police Department. Your request has been received and is being processed. Your request was received in this office on 5/22/2025 and given the reference number R158707-052225 for tracking purposes. 
Please be advised that the Austin Police Department’s Central Records Division receives over 2500 requests for information a month. These requests are processed in the order received, and we strive to respond to each request promptly, as required by law. 
Please know that, as a result of the large volume of requests received, the anticipated timeframe for responding to your request may take 10 business days if it is simply an offense report you're requesting.  If you are requesting body cam, dash cam, or other related records your request could take up to 30 days. 
You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records requested. PLEASE NOTE: The Texas Public Information Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Austin Public Records Center. 
Austin Police Department

title: Department of Defense support for upcoming film "Hotel Tehran"

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Department of Defense support for upcoming film "Hotel Tehran"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any documents pertaining to collaboration between the Department of Defense (DoD) and the production of upcoming movie "Hotel Tehran" directed by Guy Moshe. Such documents may include but should not be limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; and all records of communications between employees of DoD and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Hotel Tehran," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

"Hotel Tehran" appears on a list of DoD entertainment industry collaborations released to me through FOIA. The list is attached to this request.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: Open Records Requst: ALPR Audit

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Requst: ALPR Audit
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of June 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of June 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

communications.subject: Flock Audit Requests
communications.communication:
Good morning,

The Dublin Police Department has received your request for Flock audit for June 1,2024 to May 28, 2025. Due to the amount of data needing to be recovered and formatted there will be a copying/preparation fee of $21.44 per hour and a fee of $0.40 per disc due to the reports being too large to send via email. A shipping fee will also be required. I cannot give you an estimated total until I complete the request. Please advise if you wish to continue. Also a copy of our payment worksheet is attached for further clarification.

Thank you


[cid:image003.png@01DBD91E.CB30A250]
Latoya Jones
Police Clerk
City of Dublin
P.O. Box 690
Dublin, GA 31040
O:
478-277-5023<tel:478-277-5023>

title: Ge Death Records

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Ge Death Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records pertaining to the detention, care, death, and post-death inquiry for the person listed by ICE its death notification as Chaofeng Ge, (DOB: 11/4/1992  ; DOD: 8/5/2025), who died by alleged suicide in ICE custody at the Moshannon Valley ICE Processing Center, within the AOR of the Philadelphia ERO Field Office. See 
https://www.ice.gov/doclib/foia/reports/ddrChaofengGe.pdf

This request includes, but is not limited to, the following records: 

1. Any Mortality Review  and associated working papers conducted by ICE Health Services Corps, including any closeout report from the visit by IHSC investigators, and any preliminary mortality review record.
2. Any Death Review conducted by ICE ERAU, and all working papers relied upon to complete such review.
3. Any Healthcare and Security Compliance Analysis or similar document created by Creative Corrections or another Contractor, and all working papers relied upon to complete such review.
4. All extant video showing the decedent in custody.
5. All medical records for the decedent. 
6. Any Executive Summary or Findings information regarding the decedent's death
7. Any autopsy report for the decedent, complete with toxicology. 
8. Records of any contract discrepancy reports, Success Action Plan, contract modification, or similar findings or documents arising out of inspections or findings following the decedent's death.
9. All Significant Incident Reports for this death. 
10. All Significant Event Notifications for this death.


I request expedited processing of this request pursuant to 6 C.F.R. 5.5(e) and declare under penalty of perjury pursuant to 28 USC 1746 that the following statements are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and understanding: 

1. I am a member of the news media investigating and reporting edited pieces on deaths in ICE custody and abuses of power by federal law enforcement agencies. See www.MuckRack.com/Andrew-Free.
2. ICE deaths reached a 20-year high in 2025. 
3. There is an urgent need to inform the public of the circumstances surrounding this death in custody, and any remedial measures ICE or DHS took to address breakdowns in the delivery of medical care prior to his death.
4. The Atlanta ICE ERO Field Office and the Stewart Detention Center have been one of the two deadliest facilities in the U.S. since 2017, and CoreCivic has a disproportionately high number of deaths in custody. OPR-ERAU, IHSC, DHS-OIG, CRCL, and Congress have all previously noted CoreCivic's violation of detention standards and contractual requirements in other suicides at Stewart, dating back to May 15, 2017. 
5. An urgent need exists for the public to obtain, review, and understand the circumstances surrounding this death in custody as ICE deaths continue to rise to their highest levels since the Covid pandemic and the agency is poised to massively expand ICE detention capacity.
6. Deaths in ICE custody have been the subject of widespread and exceptional media interest about which there exist potential questions of government misconduct. See, e.g.:

a. https://www.npr.org/2025/10/23/nx-s1-5538090/ice-detention-custody-immigration-arrest-enforcement-dhs-trump
b. https://www.americanimmigrationcouncil.org/blog/trump-deadlier-for-ice-detainees-than-covid-19-pandemic/
c. http://min.house.gov/media/press-releases/reps-min-chu-lead-43-representatives-letter-demanding-answers-systemic-pattern
d. https://www.latimes.com/california/story/2025-11-22/ice-custody-deaths-raise-congress-member-questions-ismael-ayala-uribe
e. https://www.migrationpolicy.org/article/trump-immigrant-detention
f. https://wearecasa.org/dc-press-conference-honors-lives-lost-to-ice-detention-the-highest-death-toll-in-decades/

communications.subject: ICE FOIA 2026-ICFO-08700
communications.communication:
01/07/2026 

Andrew  Free 
MuckRock News, DEPT MRxxxxxx 
Boston, Massachusetts 02115 

RE:	ICE FOIA Case Number 2026-ICFO-08700 
 
Dear Requester: 

This acknowledges receipt of your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), dated 12/15/2025, and to your request for expedited treatment. Your request was received in this office on 12/15/2025. Specifically, you requested records pertaining to Chaofeng Ge who die in ICE's custody. 
Your request for expedited treatment is hereby denied. 

Under the DHS FOIA regulations, expedited processing of a FOIA request is warranted if the request involves “circumstances in which the lack of expedited treatment could reasonably be expected to pose an imminent threat to the life or physical safety of an individual,” 6 C.F.R. § 5.5(e)(1)(i), or “an urgency to inform the public about an actual or alleged federal government activity, if made by a person primarily engaged in disseminating information,” 6 C.F.R. § 5.5(e)(l)(ii).  Requesters seeking expedited processing must submit a statement explaining in detail the basis for the request, and that statement must be certified by the requester to be true and correct.  6 C.F.R. § 5.5(e)(3). 

Your request for expedited processing is denied because you do not qualify for either category under 6 C.F.R. § 5.5(e)(1).  You failed to demonstrate a particular urgency to inform the public about the government activity involved in the request beyond the public’s right to know about government activity generally.  Your letter was conclusory in nature and did not present any facts to justify a grant of expedited processing under the applicable standards. 

Due to the increasing number of FOIA requests received by this office, we may encounter some delay in processing your request. Per Section 5.5(a) of the DHS FOIA regulations, 6 C.F.R. Part 5, ICE processes FOIA requests according to their order of receipt. Although ICE’s goal is to respond within 20 business days of receipt of your request, the FOIA does permit a 10- day extension of this time period. As your request seeks numerous documents that will necessitate a thorough and wide-ranging search, ICE will invoke a 10-day extension for your request, as allowed by Title 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(B). If you care to narrow the scope of your request, please contact our office. We will make every effort to comply with your request in a timely manner. 

Provisions of the FOIA allow us to recover part of the cost of complying with your request.  We shall charge you for records in accordance with the DHS Interim FOIA regulations, as they apply to media requesters.  As a media requester, you will be charged 10 cents per page for duplication; the first 100 pages are free.  We will construe the submission of your request as an agreement to pay up to $25.00. You will be contacted before any further fees are accrued. 

If you deem the decision to deny expedited treatment of your request an adverse determination, you have the right to appeal.  Should you wish to do so, you must send your appeal and a copy of this letter, within 90 days of the date of this letter following the procedures outlined in the DHS FOIA regulations at 6 C.F.R. Part 5 § 5.5(e)(2). You may submit your appeal electronically at GILDFOIAAppeals@ice.dhs.gov or via regular mail to: 

U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement 
Office of the Principal Legal Advisor 
U.S. Department of Homeland Security 
500 12th Street,, S.W., Mail Stop 5900 
Washington, D.C. 20536-5900 

Your envelope and letter should be marked “FOIA Appeal.”  Copies of the FOIA and DHS regulations are available at www.dhs.gov/foia. 

ICE has queried the appropriate program offices within ICE for responsive records. If any responsive records are located, they will be reviewed for determination of releasability. Please be assured that one of the processors in our office will respond to your request as expeditiously as possible. We appreciate your patience as we proceed with your request. 

If you have any questions, please contact the FOIA office or Daniel Edgington, FOIA Public Liaison at 500 12th St. SW Stop 5009 Washington, DC 20536-5009 or (866) 633-1182. Additionally, you have a right to seek dispute resolution services from the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) which mediates disputes between FOIA requesters and Federal agencies as a non-exclusive alternative to litigation.  If you are requesting access to your own records (which is considered a Privacy Act request), you should know that OGIS does not have the authority to handle requests made under the Privacy Act of 1974.  You may contact OGIS as follows:  Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road-OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001, e-mail at ogis@nara.gov; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448. 

Your request has been assigned tracking number 2026-ICFO-08700. Please use this number in future correspondence. 

Sincerely, 

ICE FOIA Office 
Immigration and Customs Enforcement 
Freedom of Information Act Office 
500 12th Street, S.W., Stop 5009 
Washington, D.C. 20536-5009

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #2026-ICFO-08700
communications.communication: I appeal. There is no active law enforcement investigation which could lead to any form of civil or criminal proceeding, because ICE does not conduct such investigations in the wake of in-custody deaths, and ICE prevents local and state and federal law enforcement from conducting one.  Further, even if such investigation did exist, the blanket withholding of all materials violates the agency’s reasonable segregation obligations.

Dear Requester: 

The Department of Homeland Security has received your letter appealing the adverse determination of your Freedom of Information Act/Privacy Act (FOIA/PA) request by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement. Your appeal, dated on 1/13/2026, was received on 1/29/2026. 

The Government Information Law Division acknowledges your appeal request of and is assigning it number 2026-ICAP-00160 for tracking purposes.  Please reference this number in any future communications about your appeal. 

A high number of FOIA/PA requests have been received by the Department. While we will make every effort to process your appeal on a timely basis, there may be some delay in resolving this matter.  Should you have any questions concerning the processing of your appeal, please contact Daniel Edgington ICE FOIA Office/Public Liaison at (866) 633-1182 or at 500 12th St., SW Washington, DC 20536-5009.

Sara Jazayeri

title: Audit letters of compliance for Danbury, CT 2018-2024

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: Audit letters of compliance for Danbury, CT 2018-2024
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Management Letters: All final "Management Letters" or "Letters to Those Charged with Governance" issued by the City’s external auditors (including but not limited to CliftonLarsonAllen LLP) for the fiscal years ending 2018 through 2025.

2. Internal Control Deficiencies: Any written communications from external auditors regarding "significant deficiencies" or "material weaknesses" in the City’s internal controls related to departmental compliance with the City Charter, specifically regarding the qualification of officers under Charter Section 2-5.

3. Compliance Testing Records: Any audit "working papers" or summaries in the City's possession that document the testing of Oaths of Office for the Health Department Directors (Fernanda Carvalho, Kara Prunty, and Lisa Morrissey).

4. Single Audit Compliance: For the years 2018–2024, please provide the "Report on Compliance for Each Major State/Federal Program" that specifically addresses the Health Department’s adherence to administrative legal requirements.

Public Interest & Urgency: These records are of vital public interest following the $17 million verdict in Girolametti v. Danbury, which established a pattern of "reckless disregard" within City departments. The failure of three consecutive Health Directors to file mandatory Oaths of Office suggests a systemic failure of internal controls that the auditors were tasked to identify.

I request a response within the statutory four business days. If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific exemption and provide the name of the official responsible for the denial.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kevin Paige

Please be advised that per the Connecticut Freedom of Information Commission's strict 30-day jurisdictional window, I will be filing a formal Notice of Appeal if these records are not received by February 27, 2026. I would prefer to resolve this via this portal, but I must act to preserve my state-mandated appeal rights.

title: Request log, record retention schedule and forms

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Request log, record retention schedule and forms
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

- all record retention schedules
- master list of forms
- a log of all requests submitted to the Massachusetts State Athletic Commission, from the earliest available date until this request is fulfilled. Please include the date of request, the requester's name, the requester's organizational affiliation, a description of the request and the date the request was filled.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Chris Amico

communications.subject: FW: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Request log, record retention schedule and forms
communications.communication:
Hi Chris,
We received your public records request and have questions before we are able to fulfill. Can you help specify:
- master list of forms
- a log of all requests submitted to the Massachusetts State Athletic Commission, from the earliest available date until this request is fulfilled.

We are not sure what you mean by "master list of forms" and for "log of all requests" are you referring to public records requests?

Thank you.
Cheers,
Michele

Michele J. Campbell
Director of Communications
Office of Consumer Affairs and Business Regulation
1 Federal Street, Suite 720
Boston, MA 02110-2012

communications.subject: RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Request log, record retention schedule and forms
communications.communication:
Hi--

Thanks for the quick reply. Yes, I meant a log of public records requests. Sorry if that was unclear. 

For the master list of forms, I'm looking for something like this form that the California State Athletic Commission produces: https://www.documentcloud.org/documents/26050334-csac_forms_tracking/. I don't know if there's a better term for it.

Very much appreciate your help. Please let me know if you have other questions.

Best,
Chris

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Polymarket
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:
- Copies of all inquiries/communications.complaints to the NY Office of the Attorney General mentioning or regarding "Polymarket" or "Poly Market" submitted between June 1, 2020 and the time this request is processed.

I am a news reporter for WIRED (wired.com), so the requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. Therefore, I request that any fees are waived. But in the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available.

I am also asking for my request to be expedited. The documents I am requesting are newsworthy, given the current news-cycle concerning Polymarket as it relates to bets on Venezuela:
https://www.wsj.com/world/americas/maduro-polymarket-bet-a2e5d100
https://www.forbes.com/sites/siladityaray/2026/01/07/why-polymarket-is-not-paying-bets-on-the-us-invading-venezuela/

If you need to contact me, you can reach me at caroline_haskins@wired.com, or here. Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Caroline

communications.subject: [NYS OAG FOIL Request Center] General FOIL Request :: G000017-010726
communications.communication:
February 27, 2026 RE:  Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) Request Reference # G000017-010726 Dear Caroline Haskins: This email is in response to the above-­referenced FOIL request. The Office of the Attorney General (“OAG”) has conducted a diligent search for the records that you have requested. Please access this request in the NYS OAG FOIL Request Center to retrieve records that respond to your request.  Please be advised that some of the records that respond to your request have been withheld in their entirety, or portions have been redacted and appear as blacked out in the documents produced to you.  These records, or portions of records, are exempt from disclosure pursuant to: • Public Officers Law § 87(2)(b), because their disclosure would constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy; • Public Officers Law § 87(2)(e), because the documents requested were compiled for law-enforcement purposes and would, if disclosed, interfere with law-enforcement investigations or judicial proceedings; and • Public Officers Law § 87(2)(g), because the records are inter-agency or intra-agency materials. 
You have a right to appeal the foregoing decision.  If you elect to do so, you must appeal in writing, within 30 days, to Kathryn Sheingold, Records Appeals Officer.  Your appeal can be sent by email to FOIL.Appeals@ag.ny.gov or by mail to State of New York, Office of the Attorney General, Division of Appeals and Opinions, The Capitol, Albany, New York 12224.  You can reach the Records Appeals Officer by telephone at (518) 776-2009. Please note that records, or portions thereof, that respond to your request are drawn from the opinions, statements, or other content provided by a complainant and do not necessarily reflect the views of the OAG.  You should not construe any information provided to you by this Office to imply the Attorney General’s endorsement, recommendation, or disapproval of a given entity. 

Any numerical or other data provided by the OAG is compiled by sources believed to be reliable.  However, because of the dynamic nature of this Office’s receipt and processing of complaint information, we cannot guarantee that this information is perfect. 

When you review complaint information, you may wish to consider the entity’s size and its volume of business transactions.  You should also note the nature of the complaints and an entity’s responses to them, because that information may be equally, if not more, important than the number of complaints received by the OAG. 
This is our second and final response to the above-referenced request. Accordingly, this FOIL request is now closed. Sincerely, Records Access Officer 
 
Please visit the NYS OAG FOIL Request Center to view frequently asked questions, monitor request progress, and submit future requests. 
 View this request in the My Request Center.

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Tangipahoa Parish Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Louisiana Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Tangipahoa Parish Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Louisiana Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Department of Defense support for "Fox NFL Sunday Veteran's Day Weekend Special"

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Department of Defense support for "Fox NFL Sunday Veteran's Day Weekend Special"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the Department of Defense (DoD) and the production of the "Fox NFL Sunday Veteran's Day Weekend Special" in 2024. Such documents may include but should not be limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of DoD and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Fox NFL Sunday Veteran's Day Weekend Special," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

"Fox NFL Sunday Veteran's Day Weekend Special" appears on a list of DoD entertainment industry collaborations released to me through FOIA. The list is attached to this request.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: BWC for Jessica Copeland

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: BWC for Jessica Copeland
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I hope you are doing well.
I am requesting the following records related to this case:
	•	Body-worn camera (BWC) footage
	•	CCTV / surveillance footage
	•	Incident reports
	•	Investigation footage
Case details:
	•	Name: Jessica Copeland
	•	Incident / Arrest Date: August 6, 2025

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Martin Brooks

title: Michael Ryan Davis (aka Mike Davis) (Federal Bureau of Investigation)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Michael Ryan Davis (aka Mike Davis) (Federal Bureau of Investigation)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All files on Michael Ryan Davis, professionally known as Mike Davis, a political activist, writer, and historian who wrote frequently about California. Davis lived March 10, 1946 – October 25, 2022, and his death was widely reported in the media.

https://www.npr.org/2022/10/26/1131578207/writer-truck-driver-meatcutter-and-prophet-of-compassion-mike-davis-dies-at-76

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jacob Silverman

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL EMAIL] - RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Michael Ryan Davis (aka Mike Davis) (Federal Bureau of Investigation)
communications.communication:
Thank you for contacting foipaquestions@fbi.gov.  Please check the status of your FOIPA Request at http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on “Check Status of Your FOI/PA Request” on the right side of the page, and follow the instructions below.

Check the Status of Your FOIPA Request
If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-0] please enter [1234567-000] into the system. If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-1] please enter [1234567-001] into the system. If you have any questions about the status of your FOIPA request, please e-mail foipaquestions@fbi.gov.

FIND STATUS OF FOIPA- Request statuses are updated weekly

Please enter the whole FOIPA number-Example: [1234567-000]

FOIPA:
Results will show the Request Number, Case Type and Process Description shown below:
FOIPA:
1234567-000

Case Type:
FOIPA

Process Description (Will display the current progress of the request)
The FBI’s FOIPA Program is searching the FBI’s indices for potentially responsive documents.
You may be contacted via formal letter for all fees and/or negotiation issues that may apply.

NOTE: Recent requests are entered into the FOIPA database in the order that they are received. Before you can check the status, you must have received correspondence assigning a FOIPA request number and the information transferred to the online database. Status information is updated weekly. If a request has been closed within the last six months the online database will display the following: The FOIPA number entered has been closed, and appropriate correspondence has been sent to the address on file.

Estimated Dates of Completion
Requests are processed in the order in which they are received through our multi-track processing system.  Requests are divided into two primary tracks--simple (under 50 pages of potentially responsive documents) and complex (over 50 pages of potentially responsive documents).  Complex requests are further divided into medium, large, and extra-large sub-tracks based upon request size.  Simple track requests typically require the least amount of time to process.  Currently, simple track cases average approximately 122 days from the date of receipt for processing.  Our complex requests in the medium processing track are currently averaging 1,230 days, large processing track are currently averaging approximately 2,010 days, and extra-large processing track are currently averaging 2,370 days for processing.

Respectfully,

Public Information Officer
FBI - Information Management Division
200 Constitution Drive
Winchester, VA  22602
O: (540) 868-4593
E: foipaquestions@fbi.gov<mailto:foipaquestions@fbi.gov>

Do you have further questions about the FOI/PA process? Visit us at http://www.fbi.gov/foia

Please check the status of your request online at https://vault.fbi.gov/fdps-1/@@search-fdps    Status updates are performed on a weekly basis.

Note:  This is a non-emergency email address.  If this is an emergency, please call 911 directly.  If you need to report a tip for immediate action, please contact FBI Tips at http://tips.fbi.gov/ or reach out to your local field office.
