title: Ivan Illich (Central Intelligence Agency)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Ivan Illich (Central Intelligence Agency)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records relating to or mentioning Ivan Illich AKA Ivan Ilich (4 September 1926 – 2 December 2002), a Croatian-Austrian philosopher, Roman Catholic priest, and critic of the institutions of modern Western culture, who addressed contemporary practices in education, medicine, work, energy use, transportation, and economic development. Monsignor Ilich, of the New York Archdiocese, was accused of spreading Communism within the Church in South America. Cidoc operated from 1966 to 1976. In 1967 Illich severed his links with the Roman Catholic church. The church summoned him after a report by the CIA, but it was reportedly mostly concerned about the readership of pieces on the church. 

I am a member of the news media and request classification as such. I have previously written about the government and its activities, with some reaching over 100,000 readers. As such, as I have a reasonable expectation of publication and my editorial and writing skills are well established. In addition, I discuss and comment on the files online and make them available through non-profits such as the Internet Archive and MuckRock, disseminating them to a large audience. While my research is not limited to this, a great deal of it, including this, focuses on the activities and attitudes of the government itself. As such, it is not necessary for me to demonstrate the relevance of this particular subject in advance.

Additionally, case law states that “proof of the ability to disseminate the released information to a broad cross- section of the public is not required.” Judicial Watch, Inc. v. Dep’t of Justice, 365 F.3d 1108, 1126 (D.C. Cir. 2004); see Carney v. U.S. Dep’t of Justice, 19 F.3d 807, 814-15 (2d Cir. 1994). Further, courts have held that "qualified because it also had “firm” plans to “publish a number of . . . ‘document sets’” concerning United States foreign and national security policy." Under this criteria, as well, I qualify as a member of the news media.

Additionally, courts have held that the news media status "focuses on the nature of the requester, not its request. The provision requires that the request be “made by” a representative of the news media. Id. § 552(a)(4)(A)(ii)(II). A newspaper reporter, for example, is a representative of the news media regardless of how much interest there is in the story for which he or she is requesting information." As such, the details of the request itself are moot for the purposes of determining the appropriate fee category.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Emma Best

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Carlos Federico Diaz-Alejandro
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all files on Cuban-American economist Carlos Federico Diaz-Alejandro (July 18, 1937 – July 17, 1985). He sometimes appeared under the names Carlos F. Diaz-Alejandro or Carlos Federico Diaz Alejandro as well. His death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find his New York Times obituary at the following link: https://www.nytimes.com/1985/07/20/nyregion/prof-carlos-diaz-alejandro-expert-on-latin-economics.html

If any potentially responsive records have been destroyed and/or transferred to NARA, then I request copies of the destruction or transfer slips as well as any other documentation relating to, mentioning or describing said transfer or destruction, to include but not be limited to confirmation that your agency has no other copies of said records. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #NRC2024138265
communications.communication:
To whom this may concern,

I hope this message finds you well. My name is Matthew Petti and I am writing in regards to my FOIA request NRC2024138265, for records related to Cuban-American economist Carlos Federico Diaz-Alejandro. On March 22, 2024, the Freedom of Information Act & Privacy Act Unit responded with a total denial, arguing that records on Diaz-Alejandro are exempt under the (b)(6) privacy exemption. "To obtain these records your request must demonstrate proof that the subject(s) of your request is deceased," wrote Acting Chief FOIA Officer Jarrod T. Panter.

Diaz-Alejandro passed away in 1985, so privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. In my initial request, I included a link to the New York Times obituary for Diaz-Alejandro, which is also attached below. (https://www.nytimes.com/1985/07/20/nyregion/prof-carlos-diaz-alejandro-expert-on-latin-economics.html) Furthermore, I am attaching a recent article from the Yale Economic Growth Center on Diaz-Alejandro's intellectual legacy, which clearly mentions that he died. Therefore, I am appealing the Freedom of Information Act & Privacy Act Unit's decision to deny these records.

I look forward to hearing back your determination soon.

Sincerely,
Matthew Petti

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Yakima County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Yakima County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Records request Anduril Arsenal-1 sewer and water lines/ Inkstick Media

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Records request Anduril Arsenal-1 sewer and water lines/ Inkstick Media
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Under the Ohio Open Records Law, §149.43 et seq., I hereby request the following records:

Copies of records submitted by the Verdantas civil engineering firm regarding utilities serving the Arsenal-1 Hyperscale Manufacturing Facility in Pickaway County. Specifically, I am seeking all applications and construction plans for an extension of a water line and sanitary/sewer systems to serve the Anduril plant, including a proposed timeline for construction.

At a Madison Township Trustees zoning hearing in December, Amanda Spencer, the director of land development at Verdantas, described the planned utilities, saying: “As far as utilities go, we are planning an extension of a water line that will run along the roadway. It is tied in to an existing water line that was constructed with previous phases of development. It will head north on Airbase road, and it will head east on Airbase road. Eventually, it will continue south on Walnut Creek Pike and end at the southeast corner of the development. The sanitary will tie into a long planned sanitary extension by the City of Columbus that will be constructed on the south side of Airbase road, right along the top of this development.”

I first submitted this request to the Pickaway County Engineer, whose office told me to direct records requests related to water lines serving the plant to the Earnhart Hill Regional Water & Sewer District.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

I would request a prompt response to this request.  If you expect a significant delay in responding to or in fulfilling this request, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies of the requested records.

Sincerely,

Taylor Barnes, Inkstick Media

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Records request Anduril Arsenal-1 sewer and water lines/ Inkstick Media
communications.communication:
Good Afternoon,

Below is a recent request from your organization we received on Sunday  January 18, 2026. Earnhart is supplying potable water to the subject project. The City of Columbus is taking the sanitary sewer (copied on this reply). To date, we have not received water line plans from Verdantas for their proposed public water line work along Airbase Rd. We have, however, attached a related public water line project that will eventually connect to the Verdantas project that you cite. These plans are in draft form and are not to be used for bidding or construction.


Earnhart Hill Regional Water & Sewer District
Ohio Open Records Law Office
P.O. Box 151
2030 Stoneridge Drive
Circleville, OH 43113

January 18, 2026

To Whom It May Concern:

Under the Ohio Open Records Law, §149.43 et seq., I hereby request the following records:

Copies of records submitted by the Verdantas civil engineering firm regarding utilities serving the Arsenal-1 Hyperscale Manufacturing Facility in Pickaway County. Specifically, I am seeking all applications and construction plans for an extension of a water line and sanitary/sewer systems to serve the Anduril plant, including a proposed timeline for construction.

At a Madison Township Trustees zoning hearing in December, Amanda Spencer, the director of land development at Verdantas, described the planned utilities, saying: “As far as utilities go, we are planning an extension of a water line that will run along the roadway. It is tied in to an existing water line that was constructed with previous phases of development. It will head north on Airbase road, and it will head east on Airbase road. Eventually, it will continue south on Walnut Creek Pike and end at the southeast corner of the development. The sanitary will tie into a long planned sanitary extension by the City of Columbus that will be constructed on the south side of Airbase road, right along the top of this development.”

I first submitted this request to the Pickaway County Engineer, whose office told me to direct records requests related to water lines serving the plant to the Earnhart Hill Regional Water & Sewer District.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

I would request a prompt response to this request. If you expect a significant delay in responding to or in fulfilling this request, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies of the requested records.

Sincerely,

Taylor Barnes, Inkstick Media

View request history, upload responsive documents, and report problems here:
https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2181663/<https://linkprotect.cudasvc.com/url?a=https%3a%2f%2fwww.muckrock.com%2frespond%2f2181663%2f&c=E,1,nHLMYf-8_LgkMS8I_pkiajvL8z_cSDnLB3HJ1UjD0xuOPYU0ZR7Xcs95AMrNurUrOsSJANs2vWJngAVG8_mNw1Saf7RiojjRpdgp7RDQYF8,&typo=1&ancr_add=1>

Filed via MuckRock.com<https://linkprotect.cudasvc.com/url?a=https%3a%2f%2fMuckRock.com&c=E,1,SUW5_P8t1orikWtOL7VmQFJDsDck_ohnhUL35U-gX-i2ezCBAE-1uWhMmxCY-FWH3xJ8d8IKy0X53MVxAOZBaVMEQ1Cj8M913JDQML5LBfvWHAx3xg,,&typo=1&ancr_add=1>
E-mail (Preferred): 202646-03218783@requests.muckrock.com

PLEASE NOTE OUR NEW ADDRESS
For mailed responses, please address (see note):
MuckRock News
DEPT MR 202646
263 Huntington Ave
Boston, MA 02115

PLEASE NOTE: This request is not filed by a MuckRock staff member, but is being sent through MuckRock by the above in order to better track, share, and manage public records requests. Also note that improperly addressed (i.e., with the requester's name rather than "MuckRock News" and the department number) requests might be returned as undeliverable.



Louis A. McFarland, PE

General Manager

EHRWSD

2030 Stoneridge Drive

PO Box 151

Circleville, Ohio 43113

communications.subject: RE: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Records request Anduril Arsenal-1 sewer and water lines/ Inkstick Media
communications.communication:
Hello,

Thank you very much for providing me with the draft public water line project  for the Rickenbacker Parkway Waterline Extension 2025. 

Could you kindly confirm that, in the time since your last email, that you all have not received any application from Verdantas for water line plans for Arsenal-1? Has the firm submitted copies of all necessary shop drawings for material to be used on the project, copies of all necessary county or state highway permits, proof of insurance coverage for liability & property damage in the amounts required by the project specifications, and/or a valid State of Ohio, Bureau of Workers’ Compensation certificate? Does the Arsenal-1 developer have a "permit to install" from Earnhart Hill Regional Water & Sewer District? 

Thank you very much. I really appreciate the information so that I can get a sense of the timeline for this waterline extension being constructed.

best

Taylor Barnes

title: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Drug Free Zone Sentence Enhancements: Department of Correction, NY

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Drug Free Zone Sentence Enhancements: Department of Correction, NY
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all records related to persons serving or who have served a sentence for a state drug free zone law violation – under Consolidated Laws of New York, Penal 220.34. Specifically, I am requesting a list of all people currently or formerly incarcerated for a drug-free zone violation from 2015 to 6/30/2024. The list should include names, date of conviction, date of end of sentence, offenses convicted of, inmate ID, facility type (ie. state prison, local jail) and parole eligibility date.I would also like to request any demographic information that may be readily available for the above persons, such as, but not limited to, each person’s date of birth, age at arrest, current age, race, ethnicity and gender. If this time frame is overly burdensome, please provide a date range that can be fulfilled.
In addition, I am requesting a copy of any and all reports or studies generated by the state that discuss the passage and efficacy of the drug free zone law.
If there are any fees for obtaining or copying these records, please inform me should the cost exceed $100. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees as I am requesting the information on behalf of a 501(c)(3) nonprofit and the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of drug free zone laws. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes.
If possible, please provide the requested documents electronically in Excel format. However, if this is not feasible, please inform me of the available methods for obtaining these records. Further, if you expect a significant delay in responding to this request, please contact me with information concerning when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records.
If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption that justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.
Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Angel Carter

Sincerely, 

Angel Carter

title: Public Records Request: ReadyOp

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Public Records Request: ReadyOp
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

- All contracts, licenses, MOUs, MOAs, data agreements, letters of intent, State of Work documents, Scope of Work documents, and other agreement documents with ReadyOp Communications that were active any time between 1/1/2023 and 2/4/2026.

- All financial documents or records of financial transactions, exchanges or obligations related to ReadyOp Communications dated between 1/1/2023 and 2/4/2026. Responsive materials include all invoices, bills, receipts, check stubs, or any other records, including those possessed in an electronic format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

e

title: 2026 Brady List (Lincoln County Prosecuting Attorney)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: 2026 Brady List (Lincoln County Prosecuting Attorney)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request: 2026 Brady List (Lincoln County Prosecuting Attorney) 2026-0203-027
communications.communication:
In accordance with the Public Records Act, RCW 42.56.520, this message acknowledges receipt of your public records request, received by Lincoln County on February 3, 2026. This request has been assigned reference No. 2026-0203-027. Please include this code in any communications concerning this request.

Specifically, you requested:
All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

Our procedure is to locate records, review them for exemptions, and then prepare them for your inspection and/or copying.

We reasonably anticipate requiring approximately until on or about 02/20/2026 to provide records. We will follow up with you on or about that date regarding the status of your request. If public records become available in advance of this estimate, we will endeavor to produce them as they become available. Conversely, if staff encounters a need to extend our estimate, we will advise accordingly.

Finally, please be advised that there may be fees associated with the copying of public records. If your request requires payment, you will receive an invoice in your email before the records are released.

Sincerely,

Chandra Schumacher, CPA
Lincoln County Public Records Officer
PO Box 28  Davenport, WA  99122
509-725-4971   Cell 509-720-6277
cschumacher@co.lincoln.wa.us<mailto:cschumacher@co.lincoln.wa.us>

communications.communication:

We have received the following file from our Prosecuting Attorney's Office.

Pursuant to RCW 42.56.550(6), any claim or request for judicial review of our response must be filed within one year from the date of this notice which is 02/04/2026. Lincoln County does not intend to further address the request.  If you have any questions or need further information relating to this records request, please contact us within the next 30 days from 02/04/2026.

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (St. Clair County Sheriff's Department (SCCSD))

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (St. Clair County Sheriff's Department (SCCSD))
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

 The flash drive containing your requested records today. Upon review, the records you are requesting consist of 803 pages of records, making it a voluminous request under 5 ILCS 140/3.6. Therefore, you must respond within ten business days whether you want to amend your request so that it is not treated as a voluminous request. One option to amend the request may be identifying a narrow time frame for the records you are requesting. 
  
 Failure to respond within ten business days or failure to amend the request will result in fees being assessed pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/6. The Sheriff's Department has five business days upon receiving your response or five business days from the last day you may amend your request, whichever is sooner, to respond to your request. The Sheriff's Department may request an additional ten business days to comply with your request. Should you fail to accept or collect the responsive records, the Sheriff's Department may still charge you for their response pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/6, and your failure to pay will be considered a debt due and owing to the Sheriff's Department and may be collected in accordance with applicable law. 
  
 Additionally, your request may require over 8 hours of work due to the large time frame. Consistent with 5 ILCS 140/6(f), a public body may charge up to $10 for each hour spent by personnel in searching for and retrieving a requested record or examining the record for necessary redactions. No fees shall be charged for the first 8 hours spent by personnel in searching for or retrieving a requested record. Also consistent with 5 ILCS 140/6(a), a public body may charge for the medium the records are transferred on. Your records are on a 16 GB flash drive. 
  
 In accordance with 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a), you may have the right to have the determination your request as voluminous reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney general. Such Request for Review must be done within 60 calendar days of the date of the final denial. Any Request for Review must also be in writing and be accompanied by (i) a copy of the request for access to records; and (ii) any responses from the public body.  The Sheriff's Department performed a search of its records and tendered those records to this division for review before release. The records requested contain both private information and personal information. Consistent with 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(b) and 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(c), respectively, this information is exempt from disclosure under the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA). To the extent your request is made for those specific pieces of information, your request is denied. To the extent the documents you requested contain information not subject to an exemption, or no exemption is raised, your request is granted. You may access the released records on the JustFOIA portal using your FOIA Request Number and/or your security key. 
  
 The records requested also contain information which is exempt under 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(a) as being specifically prohibited from disclosure by federal or State law or rules and regulations implementing federal or State law. The records contain the following specifically prohibited information: 
  
 LEADS Information—under Section 1240.80(d) of title 20 of the Administrative Code. See 20 Ill. Adm. Code §1240.80(d). 
  
 Finally, the records also contain information which is or was prohibited from disclosure by the Juvenile Court Act of 1987, consistent with 5 ILCS 140/7.5(b)(b). For those records, your request is denied, and the documents are being withheld in full. 
  
 In accordance with 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a), you may have the right to have the partial denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney general.  Leah Bartelt, Public Access Counselor

 500 S. 2nd Street

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following Texas Public Information Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on Jan. 28, 2026. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: REQUEST 26.0261
communications.communication:
Good Afternoon,

Wise County Sheriff's Office received your request for records February 6, 2026. Your request is for use of force incidents in database or spreadsheet format. Wise County SO does not keep a spreadsheet or database record of use of force incidents. There is no compilation of use of force incidents in any format.

The Texas Public Information Act provides that a request must ask for records or information already in existence. The Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, do legal research, or answer questions. There is nothing responsive to the first portion of your request.

The second portion of your request states that if there is no database or spreadsheet format available, you'd like to receive copies of use of force documented/reported by officers. This makes your request voluminous. It would take hundreds of hours to search through each report in the time frame you set, attempting to locate use of force incidents. You would be charged according to the allowable charges set in the PIA. In accordance with Texas Government Code 552.222(b) we are requesting a clarification, or narrowing, in reference to your request.

Section 552.222 also provides that a request for information is considered withdrawn if the requestor does not respond in writing to a governmental body's written request for clarification or additional information within 61 days.

Respectfully,

Amy Roskilly
Special Services
Wise County Sheriff's Office
940-627-5971
roskillya@sheriff.co.wise.tx.us

title: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: March 11, 2024 Domestic Incident Arrest – Katrina Bills – Concord Police Department

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: March 11, 2024 Domestic Incident Arrest – Katrina Bills – Concord Police Department
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

An electronic copy of the bodycam footage and incident report regarding the Katrina Bills incident that occurred on March 11, 2024 on or around New Castle Street, Concord, NH 03301. The incident involved a domestic-related call and a subsequent arrest following officer response.

Specifically, I am seeking to obtain electronic copies of the:

1) BWC footage recorded by the first-responding trooper(s) to this incident.
2) BWC footage recorded by the arresting trooper(s) who took Katrina Bills into custody.
3) The corresponding incident report / arrest report and any related offense reports associated with this event.

To assist you in locating the footage and records, here are the key details of the incident:

Date of Event: March 11, 2024
Names: Katrina Bills (born 1988)
Location of Event: On or around New Castle St, Concord, NH 03301
Charges: Criminal threatening; domestic violence – criminal threatening; resisting arrest or detention; two counts of simple assault; two counts of domestic violence – simple assault

Please let me know if you require further information to process it.

If any aspects of this request are denied, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and please notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Johnson

Hi,

Please be advised that personal and confidential information, including names, addresses, dates of birth, social security numbers, personal email addresses, and personal telephone numbers contained within the documents have been redacted.  That information is exempt from disclosure under RSA 91-A:5, IV, because, if released, such disclosure would constitute an invasion of privacy.  See Lamy v. NH Public Utilities Commission, 152 N.H. 106 (2015) (upholding non-disclosure of residential names and addresses).  Concord Police does not have Body Worn Cameras.

Thank you
Taryn Davidson[Dancing]
Administration Technician III
Concord Police Department
35 Green Street, Concord, NH 03301
Tdavidson@concordpolice.com<mailto:Tdavidson@concordpolice.com>

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (West Virginia Secretary Of State Elections Division)

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (West Virginia Secretary Of State Elections Division)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

See the attached.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data. None.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ. None.

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (West Virginia Secretary Of State Elections Division)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Any and all memoranda of understanding (MOUs), agreements, contracts, or similar instruments between the state and the U.S. Department of Justice relating to the provision of voter registration data, voter files, or voter roll information, from January 20, 2025 to the present.

2. Any draft, unsigned, proposed, or rejected versions of such agreements.

3. Any amendments, addenda, or modifications to such agreements.

This request includes but is not limited to any document described or labeled as a "memorandum of understanding" or contract relating to voter data, voter list maintenance, or voter roll verification.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

title: CO County CVR Request (2022, 2024) (Montezuma County Clerk & Recorder's Office)

communications.subject: Colorado Open Records Act Request: CO County CVR Request (2022, 2024) (Montezuma County Clerk & Recorder's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Cast vote record (CVR) data for the following elections:
1. 2022 Nov Midterm Election
2. 2024 Nov General Election

Specifically, for each of these elections, I’m looking for the full anonymized record of votes cast in Montezuma County that is generated by the voting system.

In case it is helpful for your reference, you can find an example of these records in REDACTED format at this link on the Arapahoe County webpage: https://www.arapahoevotes.gov/transparency
If you follow the "Redacted Cast Vote Record (CVR)" link on that webpage, it will download a csv file with Arapahoe County's redacted CVR records (this: https://gis.arapahoegov.com/dl_data/cr_voter_data/redacted_cvr/REDACTED_CVR_Export_20241126111830_330948.zip.)

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aleksandra Conevska

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Secretary of State of Kentucky)

communications.subject: Kentucky Open Records Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Secretary of State of Kentucky)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Kentucky Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

title: Chief FOIA officer's recent email (National Aeronautics and Space Administration)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Chief FOIA officer's recent email (National Aeronautics and Space Administration)
communications.communication:
Dear National Aeronautics and Space Administration,

On behalf of myself and Fiat Fiendum, I hereby request the following records:

1. The one email in your chief FOIA officer's email inbox or equivalent which was received nearest and before October 19, 2024, midnight Eastern time, regardless of subject matter.
2. The one email in your chief FOIA officer's email spam folder or equivalent which was received nearest and before October 19, 2024, midnight Eastern time, regardless of subject matter.
3. The one email in your chief FOIA officer's sent email folder or equivalent which was sent nearest and before October 19, 2024, midnight Eastern time, regardless of subject matter.
4. The total full-time equivalent count of people who work for your FOIA office as of October 19, 2024.

This request is expressly limited to National Aeronautics and Space Administration's own FOIA office. Please do not refer it to any other agency or division. This request is expressly not a Privacy Act request, and no PA waiver will be provided.

Please provide the email records in EML, Maildir, MBOX, MIX, MSG, or PST format only — not PDF, Word, image, etc — including all metadata (e.g. full email headers as received/sent) and attachments, without alteration other than narrowly tailored electronic redaction of specifically claimed exemptions that do not affect any part of non exempt information (and, in particular, without rasterization).

Please provide the fourth record as a statement in your response letter, or at your option, as the most readily available record, in original electronic format as currently stored on your computer systems, which includes that information.

Please provide all records either as individual files, or as an unencrypted ZIP file, uploaded to MuckRock. Do not apply any encryption, DRM, or other restriction.

I request fee waiver, because
* Fiat Fiendum is a 501(c)(3) non-profit;
* this request is for public interest, non-commercial purposes;
* these records would give a snapshot of insight into the everyday processes of FOIA offices; and
* these records are being requested in order to conduct a government-wide audit of agencies' FOIA compliance.

In the event that you deny fee waiver, please process this request as-is, because I will not agree to any fees.

Please be sure to provide:
* 5 USC 552(a)(6)(A)(i)(II) FOIA Public Liaison information
* 5 USC 552(a)(6)(A)(i)(III)(aa) appeal information
* 5 USC 552(a)(6)(A)(i)(III)(bb) OGIS information
* 5 USC 552(a)(7)(A) tracking number
* 5 USC 552(a)(7)(B)(ii) completion ETA

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,
Sai
President, Fiat Fiendum, Inc.

P.S. Sai is my full legal name.

title: Information Review Task Force transfer (Defense Intelligence Agency)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Information Review Task Force transfer (Defense Intelligence Agency)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning or generated as part of the transfer of the Information Review Task Force from DIA to the JCS.

I am a member of the news media and request classification as such. I have previously written about the government and its activities, with some reaching over 100,000 readers in outlets such as MuckRock, Motherboard, Property of the People, AND Magazine, Unicorn Riot, and more. As such, as I have a reasonable expectation of publication and my editorial and writing skills are well established. In addition, I discuss and comment on the files online and make them available through non-profits such as the library Internet Archive and and the journalist non-profit, MuckRock, disseminating them to a large audience. While my research is not limited to this, a great deal of it, including this, focuses on the activities and attitudes of the government itself. As such, it is not necessary for me to demonstrate the relevance of this particular subject in advance.

Additionally, case law states that “proof of the ability to disseminate the released information to a broad cross- section of the public is not required.” Judicial Watch, Inc. v. Dep’t of Justice, 365 F.3d 1108, 1126 (D.C. Cir. 2004); see Carney v. U.S. Dep’t of Justice, 19 F.3d 807, 814-15 (2d Cir. 1994). Further, courts have held that "qualified because it also had “firm” plans to “publish a number of . . . ‘document sets’” concerning United States foreign and national security policy." Under this criteria, as well, I qualify as a member of the news media.

Additionally, courts have held that the news media status "focuses on the nature of the requester, not its request. The provision requires that the request be “made by” a representative of the news media. Id. § 552(a)(4)(A)(ii)(II). A newspaper reporter, for example, is a representative of the news media regardless of how much interest there is in the story for which he or she is requesting information." As such, the details of the request itself are moot for the purposes of determining the appropriate fee category.

As my primary purpose is to inform about government activities by reporting on it and making the raw data available, I request that fees be waived.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Emma Best

title: Clint Eastwood's Army personnel records

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Clint Eastwood's Army personnel records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the U.S. Army personnel records (OMPF) for a former servicemember named CLINTON EASTWOOD, JR. (DATE OF BIRTH: 05/31/1930). Mr. Eastwood was born in San Francisco, California. He served in the U.S. Army from 1951 to 1953 and achieved the rank of corporal. The requested records include performance reports, job assessments, disciplinary records, training received, discharge papers, and any other releasable records.

The public interest in Mr. Eastwood, an Academy Award-winning film actor and director, should favor disclosure of many of these records, particularly in relation to any disciplinary records, should those records exist.

Please be sure to review and release any segregable information, even if certain documents or portions of documents are withheld.

PLEASE NOTE: I am not a next of kin of this servicemember, and thus do not know Mr. Eastwood's Social Security Number. Because of this, I am ONLY requesting records releasable under the Freedom of Information Act. Please process my request for this servicemember's OMPF and other records under the Freedom of Information Act’s relevant provisions. I am not seeking any records that would be releasable under the Privacy Act.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation. These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: EXTERNAL COMMUNICATIONS - SAGE YMCA Title: External Communications - Sage YMCA (Wallingford Board of Education)

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: EXTERNAL COMMUNICATIONS - SAGE YMCA Title: External Communications - Sage YMCA (Wallingford Board of Education)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act (C.G.S. § 1-200 et seq.), I respectfully request access to the following public records:

PRIMARY RECORDS REQUESTED:
- All communications between YMCA staff and Wallingford Public Schools personnel from January 1, 2024 to present
- Documentation of any complaints, incidents, or issues involving YMCA members or programs from January 1, 2024 to present
- Records of staff training regarding confidentiality, member privacy, and professional boundaries
- Any communications with legal counsel regarding member disputes or staff conduct issues from January 1, 2024 to present

COMPREHENSIVE DISCLOSURE REQUIREMENTS:
This request includes, but is not limited to:
- All responsive records regardless of format (paper, electronic, audio, video, social media, messaging apps)
- Records stored on personal devices if used for official business
- Records in possession of contractors, consultants, or third parties acting on behalf of the agency
- Draft documents, notes, calendars, and informal communications
- Records that have been deleted but remain recoverable
- Metadata and audit logs associated with electronic records

SEARCH REQUIREMENTS:
- Search all relevant custodians, not just those specifically named
- Search all email accounts, shared drives, and backup systems
- Include records of employees no longer with the agency
- Search alternative spellings, nicknames, and partial names

PRODUCTION STANDARDS:
- Provide records in native electronic format when possible
- Do not convert searchable documents to non-searchable formats
- Preserve all metadata including timestamps and sender/recipient information
- Provide a privilege log for any claimed exemptions

If any responsive records have been destroyed, please provide:
- Documentation of the destruction
- Reason for destruction
- Date of destruction
- Authority for destruction

If you claim any exemption under the Freedom of Information Act, please:
- Identify the specific statutory exemption
- Explain how the exemption applies to each document
- Provide redacted copies where partial disclosure is possible
- Consider whether public interest outweighs any claimed exemption

This request seeks records that serve the public interest in government transparency and accountability. Please construe all exemptions narrowly and err on the side of disclosure.

Please preserve all potentially responsive records immediately upon receipt of this request to prevent spoliation.

As these records serve significant public interest, please waive all fees. If fees cannot be waived, please provide detailed cost estimate before proceeding.

Thank you for your assistance.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: EXTERNAL COMMUNICATIONS - SAGE YMCA Title: External Communications - Sage YMCA (Wallingford Board of Education)
communications.communication:
Hello,

It looks like this request was going to an old email address, so we've redirected the request to this contact.

---

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act (C.G.S. § 1-200 et seq.), I respectfully request access to the following public records:

PRIMARY RECORDS REQUESTED:
- All communications between YMCA staff and Wallingford Public Schools personnel from January 1, 2024 to present
- Documentation of any complaints, incidents, or issues involving YMCA members or programs from January 1, 2024 to present
- Records of staff training regarding confidentiality, member privacy, and professional boundaries
- Any communications with legal counsel regarding member disputes or staff conduct issues from January 1, 2024 to present

COMPREHENSIVE DISCLOSURE REQUIREMENTS:
This request includes, but is not limited to:
- All responsive records regardless of format (paper, electronic, audio, video, social media, messaging apps)
- Records stored on personal devices if used for official business
- Records in possession of contractors, consultants, or third parties acting on behalf of the agency
- Draft documents, notes, calendars, and informal communications
- Records that have been deleted but remain recoverable
- Metadata and audit logs associated with electronic records

SEARCH REQUIREMENTS:
- Search all relevant custodians, not just those specifically named
- Search all email accounts, shared drives, and backup systems
- Include records of employees no longer with the agency
- Search alternative spellings, nicknames, and partial names

PRODUCTION STANDARDS:
- Provide records in native electronic format when possible
- Do not convert searchable documents to non-searchable formats
- Preserve all metadata including timestamps and sender/recipient information
- Provide a privilege log for any claimed exemptions

If any responsive records have been destroyed, please provide:
- Documentation of the destruction
- Reason for destruction
- Date of destruction
- Authority for destruction

If you claim any exemption under the Freedom of Information Act, please:
- Identify the specific statutory exemption
- Explain how the exemption applies to each document
- Provide redacted copies where partial disclosure is possible
- Consider whether public interest outweighs any claimed exemption

This request seeks records that serve the public interest in government transparency and accountability. Please construe all exemptions narrowly and err on the side of disclosure.

Please preserve all potentially responsive records immediately upon receipt of this request to prevent spoliation.

As these records serve significant public interest, please waive all fees. If fees cannot be waived, please provide detailed cost estimate before proceeding.

Thank you for your assistance.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

john Conrad

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: AB1388 (San Diego County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Per AB 1388, I am seeking the department's separation agreements made with officers. The records may be considered non-disclosure agreements or clean-record agreements.  These agreements are now void and must be disclosed under state law. https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/billTextClient.xhtml?bill_id=202520260AB1388 

Please provide me with copies of these agreements, on a rolling basis as records are ready for release, that cover 2012 to today's date. 

"This bill would additionally exempt agreements between an employing agency and a peace officer that, among other things, require the agency to destroy, remove, or conceal a record of a misconduct investigation. The bill would declare that its provisions are severable."

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

title: MA enterprise agreement with OpenAI

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: MA enterprise agreement with OpenAI
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Any documents related to a government contract with OpenAI referencing the enterprise-wide deployment of a ChatGPT-powered AI assistant. Documents could include the contract and/or enterprise agreement between Massachusetts and OpenAI.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aarushi Sahejpal

communications.subject: RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: MA enterprise agreement with OpenAI
communications.communication:
Good afternoon Aarushi,

You can find the OpenAI contract documents here:

https://www.commbuys.com/bso/external/purchaseorder/poSummary.sda?docId=PO-19-1080-OSD03-SRC01-17283&releaseNbr=31&parentUrl=close

Please let me know if you have any questions.

Thank you,

Michelle
617-447-1754

title: January 2026 agency emails related to 2471 Concord Street

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: January 2026 agency emails related to 2471 Concord Street
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all agency emails (and attachments) related to 2471 Concord Street, parcel # 15011708, from January 1-31, 2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tracie McMillan

communications.subject: Re: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: January 2026 agency emails related to 2471 Concord Street
communications.communication:
Hello, Traci -

Thank you for contacting the Detroit Land Bank Authority’s Freedom of Information Office. Your FOIA request is deemed received today, February 6, 2026. We will review your request and respond accordingly.



[cid:ee42d6e4-c95d-4780-91a1-f2fec4c33697]

Thank you and kind regards,


Patricia Kelly

FOIA Coordinator


500 Griswold St, Suite 1200 | Detroit, MI 48226

foia@detroitlandbank.org | www.buildingdetroit.org<http://www.buildingdetroit.org/>


WARNING:  This communication (including any attachments) may include, in whole or in part, information protected by the attorney-client privilege, the attorney work product doctrine, the deliberative process privilege, or by other privilege. This communication is confidential and intended solely for receipt by the individual or entity to whom it is addressed.  If you are not the intended recipient, any use of the communication is neither allowed nor intended.  If you receive this email in error, please securely delete the email (including any attachments) and notify the originator that you received the message in error.

communications.subject: 2471 Concord - FOIA Payment
communications.communication:
Hi Tracie McMillan –

We have completed review of your FOIA request, dated January 5, 2026, wherein you requested, “Any and all agency emails (and attachments) related to 2471 Concord Street, parcel # 15011708, from December 1-31, 2025.”  Please contact me at FOIA@detroitlandbank.org<mailto:FOIA@detroitlandbank.org> or at (313) 989-4638 to schedule an appointment to submit the balance of $38.34 owed for this request.  Once received, the DLBA will email you copies of records in its possession that are responsive to your request.

Thank you,

Adela Bejo
Interim FOIA Coordinator

title: Florida DJJ Youth Pay to Stay Policy

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Florida DJJ Youth Pay to Stay Policy
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

The following materials regarding the imposition, assessment, collection and balances of fees and debt for incarcerated youth (i.e., juvenile):

a) All current policies and procedures regarding medical and room & board costs or fees for which incarcerated youth (i.e. juvenile) and/or their parents or legal guardians are or may become liable. Please include all policies and procedures related to how fees are set, accrued, imposed, collected, and enforced (such as those related to per diem fees, ability to pay determinations, penalties for late or non-payment, authorization for civil or criminal action for payment enforcement, etc).

b) All reports, databases, spreadsheets, or equivalent materials regarding medical copays and room and board fees paid to or owed to this department/office or any facility/agency under its jurisdiction in FY2024. Please include all relevant data regarding medical and room & board costs, including the number of youths (i.e., juveniles) charged for medical and room & board costs or fees and the total amount of medical and room & board fees imposed in FY2024.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Jenny Wadhwa
